concepts.title: Causes of the Crisis of the Constitutional Order
concepts.description: The crisis was caused by the growing gap between the Constitution and the emerging reality in India, leading to erosion of its principles and institutions, and the rise of authoritarian tendencies in the country.

concepts.title: Features of the Crisis
concepts.description: The crisis is characterized by erosion of civil liberties, rise of authoritarianism, decline of democratic institutions, and the concentration of power in the hands of the executive.

concepts.title: Constitutional Amendments during the Crisis
concepts.description: The 42nd Amendment, 1976, and the 55th Amendment, 1980, further eroded the civil liberties and the independence of the judiciary, marking a significant slide towards authoritarianism.

concepts.title: Key Events of the Crisis
concepts.description: INDRA - Internal Emergency (1975), National Emergency (1975-1977), Decline of democratic institutions, Rise of authoritarianism.

concepts.title: Misconception about the Emergency
concepts.description: Students often confuse the Internal Emergency and the National Emergency. The Internal Emergency was declared on June 25, 1975, whereas the National Emergency was proclaimed on June 26, 1975.

concepts.title: Key Players during the Crisis
concepts.description: Indira Gandhi, Sanjay Gandhi, Morarji Desai, Jagjivan Ram, Charan Singh, Jayaprakash Narayan, and Atal Bihari Vajpayee were key players in the crisis of the constitutional order.

concepts.title: End of the Crisis
concepts.description: The crisis of the constitutional order came to an end with the restoration of democracy in 1977, following the general elections in which the Janata Party came to power, defeating the Congress Party.

concepts.title: Impact of the Crisis
concepts.description: The crisis had a profound impact on Indian democracy, leading to a decline in civil liberties, erosion of democratic institutions, and the rise of authoritarianism, which has continued to shape Indian politics even after the restoration of democracy.
